mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals accelerating labor market recovery, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives immediate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE drops > 0.3pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with increasing housing starts indicate synchronized demand expansion across durable goods and real estate, which lifts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the industrial production index above its 12-month moving average during a post-recession phase confirms supply-side capacity utilization recovery, which sustains positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month MA during the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reduces recession risk premiums and lowers equity risk premia, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants price in stronger aggregate demand.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth rates remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.4 percent triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of months where CPIAUCSL increases > 0.4% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market tightening, which accelerates wage-driven consumption and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to outperform a random baseline in predicting 20-day SPX returns across three consecutive calendar years.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index below 98.0 during a peak capacity utilization phase signals supply chain bottlenecks and input cost shocks, which erodes corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: peak capacity utilization with supply constraints
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO falls below 98.0 during peak capacity utilization periods (t-test p > 0.05).
